tossups.question: In a novel primarily set in this city, a doctor reflects on how Nietzsche imitated Mucius Scaevola after Moji reminds him of how he sexually assaulted her at a party 20 years prior. The documentary The 50 Year Argument narrates the history of a magazine based in this city. The only surviving shop from this city’s old “Book Row” is the massive Strand Bookstore. This city names a company that often publishes previously untranslated and out-of-print novels under its “Classics" imprint, as well as an (*) oft-abbreviated literary magazine. During “the summer they electrocuted the Rosenbergs,” Esther Greenwood interns at a magazine in this city in Sylvia Plath’s The Bell Jar. For 10 points, name this city whose publishers include Columbia University Press.
tossups.answer: New York City [or NYC; or New York Review of Books; or NYRB] (The first sentence is from Teju Cole’s Open City; The 50 Year Argument was directed by Martin Scorsese.)
tossups.answer_sanitized: New York City [or NYC; or New York Review of Books; or NYRB] (The first sentence is from Teju Cole's Open City; The 50 Year Argument was directed by Martin Scorsese.)
tossups.question_sanitized: In a novel primarily set in this city, a doctor reflects on how Nietzsche imitated Mucius Scaevola after Moji reminds him of how he sexually assaulted her at a party 20 years prior. The documentary The 50 Year Argument narrates the history of a magazine based in this city. The only surviving shop from this city's old "Book Row" is the massive Strand Bookstore. This city names a company that often publishes previously untranslated and out-of-print novels under its "Classics" imprint, as well as an (*) oft-abbreviated literary magazine. During "the summer they electrocuted the Rosenbergs," Esther Greenwood interns at a magazine in this city in Sylvia Plath's The Bell Jar. For 10 points, name this city whose publishers include Columbia University Press.

tossups.question: Over footage of a film by this director, the narrator of a documentary describes how the world that centered on his school and his home on Elizabeth Street “got much bigger” watching films on TV. That narrator, an American director who briefly was part of this director’s family, added the word “my” to the title of one of their films for a four-hour documentary about the cinema of his country. That documentary also opens with one of this director’s scenes of an unconscious man floating down a (*) river on a small raft, with a sign labelling him a partisan. After some fascist-era films, Fellini’s first critically serious film credits are screenplays for this director’s “post-war trilogy”, though he would dramatically depart from this director’ style later. This director’s daughter, who starred in Death Becomes Her and Blue Velvet, was born to the Swedish star of several of his films, like Stromboli. For 10 points, identify this director of Germany, Year Zero and the father of Isabella, whose marriage to Ingrid Bergman coincided with the release of Voyage to Italy.
tossups.answer: Roberto Rosselini [The clues in power are from Martin Scorsese’s My Voyage in Italy]
tossups.answer_sanitized: Roberto Rosselini [The clues in power are from Martin Scorsese's My Voyage in Italy]
tossups.question_sanitized: Over footage of a film by this director, the narrator of a documentary describes how the world that centered on his school and his home on Elizabeth Street "got much bigger" watching films on TV. That narrator, an American director who briefly was part of this director's family, added the word "my" to the title of one of their films for a four-hour documentary about the cinema of his country. That documentary also opens with one of this director's scenes of an unconscious man floating down a (*) river on a small raft, with a sign labelling him a partisan. After some fascist-era films, Fellini's first critically serious film credits are screenplays for this director's "post-war trilogy", though he would dramatically depart from this director' style later. This director's daughter, who starred in Death Becomes Her and Blue Velvet, was born to the Swedish star of several of his films, like Stromboli. For 10 points, identify this director of Germany, Year Zero and the father of Isabella, whose marriage to Ingrid Bergman coincided with the release of Voyage to Italy.

tossups.question: In Akira Kurosawa’s 1990 film Dreams, this man has a brief cameo as Vincent van Gogh. In a three-part 1995 BBC documentary, this man broke down a director’s role into four categories: storyteller, illusionist, smuggler, and iconoclast; that documentary is titled A Personal Journey with this man through American Movies. Among this rock-and-roll-loving filmmaker’s documentaries are The Last Waltz and Shine a Light. A 2011 film directed by this man stars Asa Butterfield as a young French boy whose dead father tried to repair an automaton. Hugo was directed by, for 10 points, what American filmmaker whose collaborations with Leonardo DiCaprio include Gangs of New York and The Wolf of Wall Street?

Among this rock-and-roll-loving filmmaker's documentaries are The Last Waltz and Shine a Light. A 2011 film directed by this man stars Asa Butterfield as a young French boy whose dead father tried to repair an automaton. Hugo was directed by, for 10 points, what American filmmaker whose collaborations with Leonardo DiCaprio include Gangs of New York and The Wolf of Wall Street?

tossups.question: The subject of a documentary by this director defends her smoking habit by saying “Everything they say about smoking they said about homosexuality in the 70s” during an interview which takes place at a booth in the Waverly Inn. In a documentary by this director, a married couple jokingly argues over the fact that the husband insists on sitting at the opposite end of the couch from his wife. One of this man’s documentaries classifies directors as storytellers, illusionists, smugglers or iconoclasts. A recipe for (*) meatballs appears in the credits of the documentary this man made about his parents Catherine and Charles. This director of Public Speaking made a concert film for The Band which is sometimes considered the greatest concert film ever made. For 10 points, name this director of Italianamerican and The Last Waltz, whose fiction films include Goodfellas.

tossups.question: In one of this director’s films, the opening credits are preceded by a triple axial cut towards the protagonist’s face as his head hits his pillow. This director’s self-identified "best film" is a documentary set at an Elizabeth Street apartment featuring conversations with his parents Charles and Catherine. The protagonist of one of his films has a secret affair with his epileptic cousin and holds his hand over open flames in a restaurant kitchen and in church. This director used what he calls a "priest’s-eye view" shot to show the aftermath of a massacre in a brothel run by the pimp Sport. In that film of his, the child prostitute Iris fascinates the disturbed title character, Travis Bickle. For 10 points, name this Italian-American director of Mean Streets and Taxi Driver.
tossups.question_sanitized: In one of this director's films, the opening credits are preceded by a triple axial cut towards the protagonist's face as his head hits his pillow. In that film of his, the child prostitute Iris fascinates the disturbed title character, Travis Bickle. For 10 points, name this Italian-American director of Mean Streets and Taxi Driver.

bonuses.leadin: Sergio Leone's Once Upon a Time in the West has inspired many spiritual successors. For 10 points each:
parts: Leone's later film Once Upon a Time in America was restored by this director, whose World Cinema Project has distributed films like A Brighter Summer Day and Mysterious Object at Noon. A historical drama by this director adapted the unverified book I Heard You Paint Houses.
This director restored period details of 1969 LA but altered Sharon Tate's fate in his 2019 film about the Manson family murders, Once Upon a Time in Hollywood.
This Turkish director of the Chekhov-inspired film Winter Sleep used a group's murder investigation to frame humorous observations of quotidian rural life in Once Upon a Time in Anatolia.
answers: Martin Scorsese [or Martin Charles "Marty" Scorsese] (The historical drama was The Irishman.)
answers_sanitized: Martin Scorsese [or Martin Charles "Marty" Scorsese] (The historical drama was The Irishman.)

parts: Name this Todd Phillips film that won the Golden Lion at the 2019 Venice Film Festival. In this film, Joaquin Phoenix plays a failed stand-up comedian named Arthur Fleck, who then becomes the title DC Comics villain.

Joker was partially inspired by this director’s film The King of Comedy. This man’s most recent film eschewed the use of motion tracking markers for its extensive CGI, which was used to “de-age” its main actors.

bonuses.leadin: Name these contemporary film directors who have depicted sexual obsession, for 10 points each.
parts: Michael Fassbender played the sex addict Brandon, whose life is thrown out of balance by his sister’s return, in this British director’s film Shame. This man directed 12 Years a Slave.

In a lengthy erotic film by this director, Seligman’s accounts of fly-fishing parallel the sexual experiences of Joe, who is played by Charlotte Gainsbourg. The 2018 premiere of a film by this director about a serial killer led to several mass walkouts at Cannes (“can”).
Paul Schrader, who fictionalized the life of sex addict Bob Crane in his 2002 film Auto Focus, is better known for writing screenplays for films by this director, such as Taxi Driver and Raging Bull.
answers: Lars von Trier (The films are Nymphomaniac and The House that Jack Built.)
answers_sanitized: Lars von Trier (The films are Nymphomaniac and The House that Jack Built.)
